name: trackeros
description:
A corporate leave management system for a mid-size company.
Employees can apply for different types of leave: annual, sick, and emergency.
Managers can approve or reject leave requests with comments.
HR administrators can configure leave policies, view team calendars, and generate reports.
The system must enforce leave balance limits and prevent overlapping requests.
Built with TypeScript, Node.js, Express, PostgreSQL, and npm.
Uses Jest for testing.

rules.description: Use unknown with type guards instead of any
Database calls must go through repository pattern
No hardcoded passwords, API keys, or tokens
Do not add @gestalt/* packages as project dependencies — these are Gestalt platform internals not available on npm

rules: All database connections must be created in src/shared/db/ only. Importing a database type for use in a type signature is NOT a violation — only instantiating a connection is.
No SQL queries outside repository classes. Services, routes, and controllers must not contain SQL.
No console.log/warn/error in production source files.
All async functions must handle errors — no unhandled promise rejections.
No hardcoded credentials or connection strings in source files.
Generated code must compile without errors. Verify with executeScript before returning.
All imports must resolve to files that exist in the project or are declared in package.json.
You MUST run a compile/lint check via executeScript before emitting the final files. This is not optional.
Tests must cover every success criterion from the IntentSpec.
Only flag violations for layers explicitly in scope for this intent.
Do not flag items as missing if they already exist on the project's main branch.
Verify import resolution by running tsc --noEmit before flagging unresolved imports.
Do not flag golden principle violations for concerns excluded by IntentSpec.outOfScope.
